name: add_file
description: Add a new C# source file to an existing project: ground it in the KB and the project's own code, choose or honor a target path, generate, build the WHOLE project as the oracle, repair until green, then register the file in the project's on-disk memory (project.json + PROJECT.md).
whenToUse: Add, create, or write a new file/class/driver/adapter/feature INTO an existing project (the user names a project). Generates the file, rebuilds the whole project, repairs until it compiles, and records it.

nodes.prompt:
You are choosing the target file path for a C# source file in an existing project.

Project (layout, existing files and roles):
--- PROJECT ---
{proj_context}
--- END ---

Task: {request}

Rules:
- If the task explicitly names a target path under src\ (for example src\Drivers\Foo.cs), output THAT exact path.
- If the task describes the WHOLE program / the app's overall behavior / the entry point (what the app does when run), output src\Program.cs (this regenerates the entry point and wires things together).
- Otherwise, for one focused piece: pure domain logic -> src\Core\<Name>.cs ; an I/O adapter/driver -> src\Drivers\<Name>.cs ; a WinForms window -> src\Ui\<Name>.cs. Pick a PascalCase <Name> from the task.
- Use backslashes. The path MUST start with src\ and end with .cs.

Output ONLY the path on a single line. No prose, no markdown.

body.prompt:
Write ONE complete C# source file for an EXISTING project, to be saved at {path}. It must compile under the in-box csc at langversion 5 as part of the whole project.

Ground it in the project's existing code and conventions - reuse its namespaces and implement its interfaces/seams exactly:
--- PROJECT ---
{proj_context}
--- END ---

Relevant reference material (use these idioms):
--- REFERENCE ---
{kb_context}
--- END ---

Task: {request}

Rules:
- Use the root namespace App with the sub-namespace matching the folder of {path} (App.Core, App.Drivers, App.Ui). The entry file src\Program.cs stays in namespace App.
- ENTRY POINT: if {path} IS the entry file (src\Program.cs for a console app), you MUST include exactly one static int Main(string[] args) and WIRE IN the project's existing types (call its Core classes / use its Drivers). For any OTHER path, do NOT write a Main method - the project already has its entry point - and write only the single type the task asks for (no demo Main, no extra classes).
- If the project defines an interface this file should implement (e.g. IOutput), implement it exactly.
FORBIDDEN (will not compile under C# 5): string interpolation $"...", expression-bodied members =>, null-conditional ?., target-typed new, records, tuples. Use string concatenation or string.Format and full method/property bodies. (Ternary ?: is fine.)

If the compiler reported errors on the previous attempt, fix EXACTLY those:
{output}

Return ONLY the complete C# file (no prose, no markdown fences).

then.prompt:
In ONE short line (max ~10 words, no trailing period), describe the role of the file {path} you just wrote for this task: {request}

Output only the line.
